summary: Housekeeping PR that deleted five deprecated executable shell helper scripts under tools/: asmdiff.sh, build.sh, gen.sh, objdiff-make.sh, and resync.sh. The removed scripts wrapped older workflows for objdump-based DOL diffs, make invocations, context/m2c generation, objdiff make flags, and force-push resyncing. No replacement code, comments, or review discussion are present in the provided slice, so the main evidence is the title and deletions.
what_changed: Deleted tools/asmdiff.sh, a small bash wrapper around powerpc-eabi-objdump that dumped address ranges from baserom.dol and build/ssbm.us.1.2/main.dol and diffed them.

Deleted tools/build.sh, a larger bash build wrapper that handled make clean, selective object deletion, formatting, NON_MATCHING, GENERATE_MAP, EPILOGUE_PROCESS, SKIP_CHECK, job count, build logging, parse_map.py, dadosod dumping, and syncing build or dump outputs to expected/.
Deleted tools/gen.sh, a generation wrapper for ctx_includes.h, ctx.c via m2ctx.py, and m2c output from asm files into src paths.
Deleted tools/objdiff-make.sh, a three-line wrapper invoking make with GENERATE_MAP=1, MAX_ERRORS=1, WIP=1, parallel jobs from nproc, and WINE=wibo.
Deleted tools/resync.sh, a git automation script that committed local changes, fetched doldecomp/melee master, soft-reset to FETCH_HEAD, committed the merge, and force-pushed.
smart_moves: Removed entire deprecated scripts rather than leaving stale wrappers around build, generation, objdiff, and resync workflows.
Reduced maintenance surface in tools/ by eliminating duplicate command-line interfaces and older automation paths.
Deleted a potentially risky resync helper that automated commits, soft resets, and force-push behavior.
Kept the PR tightly scoped: all changes are file deletions with no mixed behavioral edits elsewhere.
decomp_lessons: Deprecated helper scripts should be removed once workflows move to Makefile targets, newer tools, or documented commands; stale scripts can mislead contributors about supported decomp workflows.
Build and matching helper logic should avoid being duplicated across ad hoc shell wrappers unless the wrapper is actively maintained.
Automation that mutates git history or force-pushes is especially risky in shared decomp projects and should not linger as a deprecated tool.
When cleaning tooling, small deletion-only PRs are easy to audit and reduce the chance of accidentally changing matching code or game logic.
naming_conventions: Removed shell helper names used simple lowercase hyphenated filenames under tools/: asmdiff.sh, build.sh, gen.sh, objdiff-make.sh, resync.sh.
No new symbol, function, struct, or header naming convention was introduced by this PR.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Removed an older objdump-based asmdiff tactic using DEVKITPPC/bin/powerpc-eabi-objdump with flags -D -bbinary -EB -mpowerpc -M gekko over baserom.dol and built main.dol.
Removed a build wrapper path for NON_MATCHING, GENERATE_MAP, EPILOGUE_PROCESS, SKIP_CHECK, WIP, MAX_ERRORS, and WINE=wibo flags.
Removed an m2c generation wrapper that produced .s.c files from asm paths using tools/m2c/m2c.py with ppc-mwcc-c, valid syntax, and a generated context.
Because the PR only deletes deprecated scripts, it provides no direct matching improvement or new assembly technique to reuse.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, or reviews are present in the provided PR slice.
There is no recorded reviewer rationale beyond the PR title indicating the scripts were deprecated.
follow_up_queries: What newer build or generation workflow replaced tools/build.sh and tools/gen.sh?
Where is the supported objdiff workflow documented after removal of tools/objdiff-make.sh?
Was asmdiff.sh superseded by objdiff or another comparison tool?
Are there remaining references in documentation or scripts to the deleted shell helpers?
